title: Alphabet plans to raise $80B to pay for AI buildout
description: "The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the company’s available supply," Alphabet said in its statement.

title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
description: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents
description: The lawsuit partially revolves around a shooting at Florida State University last year, and ChatGPT's alleged role in the incident.

title: Water access is now a risk factor in SpaceX’s IPO
description: The company says it needs "significant" water resources to cool its data centers, and that access to abundant, affordable water is a challenge.

title: Anthropic files to go public
description: Anthropic, now an AI powerhouse that has landed top-tier enterprise customers, was once considered an underdog in the emerging world of large language models.

title: This AI weather startup is out-forecasting government agencies
description: WindBorne benefits from its unique combination of model-building and data collection. The company now has about 400 balloons in flight gathering sensor readings at any given time, launched from 15 sites around the globe. The advances in its current model come from improvements in how the data collected by the balloons is fed into the models.

title: DuckDuckGo makes its ‘no-AI’ search engine easier to access as its traffic booms
description: Alternative search engine DuckDuckGo launches 'no AI' web extensions for Chrome and Firefox users.

title: Erin Brockovich takes aim at data center secrecy
description: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich has a new mission.

title: Making sense of the debate over AI psychosis
description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether tech CEOs are "uniquely prone to AI psychosis."

title: SoftBank says it will invest up to €75B to build French data centers
description: The goal, the firm said, is to develop and operate up to 5 gigawatts of additional data center capacity.

title: ‘What a joke’: GitHub Copilot’s new token-based billing spurs consternation among devs
description: The golden age of Microsoft's GitHub Copilot appears to be at an end.

title: Meta is reportedly developing an AI pendant
description: Meta seems to be making big bets on AI-powered hardware.

title: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
description: Gemini Spark helps automate everyday tasks, from inbox summaries to local event planning, but it’s unclear why Google made it a separate product.

title: The groupthink boom: what 3 top VCs really think about the AI frenzy
description: "If you're 22 years old in San Francisco and building something in AI, there may be a seed term sheet in your inbox — but if you're 19, oh my God, this means you're really good; you might already have a Series A [offer]," said one, half-kiddingly.

title: As the browser wars heat up, here are the hottest alternatives to Chrome and Safari in 2026
description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

title: Coders are refusing to work without AI — and that could come back to bite them
description: While AI is helping coders produce code faster, it may not be producing better code, researchers warn. And that could cause problems down the road for them.

title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

title: What happens when companies become too AI-pilled?
description: The people deciding that AI can replace your job are also the ones least likely to understand what your job truly involves, according to Box founder Aaron Levie, who pointed to this as an example of “AI psychosis.” Indeed, ClickUp recently cut 22% of its workforce for AI agents, tech layoffs in 2026 are already nearly matching all of 2025, […]

title: After Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M
description: Chipmaker Groq is looking to raise $650 million in internal funding as it pivots from hardware to focus more on AI inference, the process of refining the way AI models respond to prompted requests, per Axios.

title: Cognition’s Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn’t replace humans
description: Cognition makes Devin, the first and arguably most successful AI coding agent. But famed coder Wu says it isn't designed to supplant human programmers.
